text:
first they applies the Odd-Even divions to Yama.
One 'Ahoratra' is the time from one sunrise to the sunrise
in the next day. This time period of 'Ahoratra' (1 day and 1
night) is composed of 16 yama. These Yama are classified
into two-

~R~hy~th~m=-----------~o~( __________~ D~a~yL_ __________3 7
Odd (Oja/Male) = 1,3,5,7,9,11,13,15 Yama
Even (Yugma/Female) = 2,4,6,8,10,12,14,16 Yama
This Odd-Even division is essential for determining the
order of rising of five fundamental constituents such as Sky
Air-Fire-Water-Earth.
Pancha Bootodaya (Rise of Five Tatvas)
This is also known as Pancha Tatva concept. Pancha
Bootodaya is a concept fundamental to Ancient Indian
thought. Sankhya, Jyotisha, Ayurveda etc. all utilize this
concept. Ayurveda states that from the nature which is of
Avyakta (abstract, non-physical) nature, evolved the five
fundamental constituents. Here the word 'Nature' refers to
the original stuff (space-time continuum) with which
everything is made up of.
